surface_verification.justification: Library/CLI change to the compliance SBOM generator + collector (AR-04). No product web/api/cli/UI/store runtime surface exists in this repo (the WebUI is a separate repo); sbom.md is a static tracked markdown artifact. Verified end-to-end by running the new generator against the synced main tree AND the worktree (byte-identical clean 7/7 artifact, openai deduped to 2.30.0, ASCII-OK) plus the 794-test compliance suite green and pinned-ruff clean; anti-ratchet 0 ratchets.

behaviors.behavior: Parse uv.lock -> {canonical_name: version} (canonical names, missing file, malformed TOML)
Resolve Python version from sibling uv.lock; dedupe by installed version (openai collapses to one row); floor fallback when no lock
Dedup survives one missing/stale lock via the project-wide union fallback (code-review finding #1)
Resolve license across ALL venvs (sibling-workspace venv, installed-no-license, real-license-wins, not-installed-anywhere)
ComplianceData dedup/lock flags set by collect_all (default False without a lock)
Summary counts ALL packages: 'Licenses resolved X/Y' row, '(deduplicated)' annotation (guarded vs 0-runtime), header 'resolved from uv.lock' note
Pie counts all packages + an 'unknown' slice; license_pie back-compat for the no-kwargs callers
Honest verdict: clean / 'could not be resolved - verify' / 'declare no license'; never claims 'all permissive' while any license is unresolved
SBOM doc is ASCII-only across all branches (copyleft/no_license/not_installed/clean/empty)
Triage producer unchanged (strict per-manifest NOT_INSTALLED vs UNKNOWN semantics preserved)
